title: TRIM29 promotes epithelial–mesenchymal transition, angiogenesis, and stromal remodeling in lung adenocarcinoma: integrated validation at histologic, transcriptomic, and protein levels
task: Characterize TRIM29 function in lung adenocarcinoma progression and identify therapeutic targets
key_results: High TRIM29 expression associated with poor overall survival
TRIM29 inhibition reduced N-cadherin/SNAIL/TWIST, increased E-cadherin; mTOR inhibitors showed highest activity in TRIM29-high cells
comments: Integrated multi-level validation (transcriptomics, immunohistochemistry, cell lines) strengthens claims, though the therapeutic mechanism of mTOR inhibition relative to TRIM29 remains to be elucidated.

model_io.model: GSEA with TME deconvolution (xCell, ESTIMATE, EPIC)

hypotheses.hypothesis: TRIM29 drives EMT, angiogenesis, and stromal remodeling in LUAD
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: TRIM29-high tumors show enriched EMT signatures, increased fibroblasts/endothelial cells, and elevated stromal scores across transcriptomic and histopathologic analyses

hypotheses.hypothesis: TRIM29 inhibition suppresses EMT markers and sensitizes cells to mTOR inhibitors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: TRIM29 knockdown decreased mesenchymal markers (N-cadherin, SNAIL, TWIST) and increased epithelial marker (E-cadherin); mTOR inhibitors most effective in TRIM29-high cells

title: Enhancing survival risk prediction through imputation and feature selection in high-dimensional protein biomarker data
task: Biomarker selection and survival risk prediction from high-dimensional proteomic data
key_results: Integrates random forest imputation, LASSO-penalized Cox regression, and random survival forests; demonstrates stable prognostic biomarker identification despite overfitting challenges
comments: Workflow is practical but transparently presented as engineering best-practice rather than novel methodology; small sample + high dimensions remains inherently challenging.

model_io.model: Sequential pipeline: imputation → feature selection → survival modeling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unsupervised RF imputation reduces information leakage vs. outcome-aware methods in survival settings
hypotheses.result: Not explicitly tested; claimed but not quantitatively demonstrated

title: Design, synthesis, and in vivo antiepileptic evaluation of novel quinazolinone-phthalimide derivatives
task: Design and validate novel quinazolinone-phthalimide antiepileptic derivatives
key_results: Compounds 8j and 8k show efficacy comparable to diazepam in pentylenetetrazole and lithium-pilocarpine models; GABAA antagonism confirmed; docking shows benzodiazepine-pocket binding
comments: Chemical optimization workflow is straightforward; molecular dynamics validates binding stability but in vivo efficacy endpoint lacks detailed pharmacokinetics.

model_io.model: Structure-based drug discovery: synthesis → in vivo seizure models → molecular docking → MD validation

hypotheses.hypothesis: 8j and 8k bind GABAA benzodiazepine pocket and suppress seizures via GABAA antagonism comparable to diazepam
hypotheses.result: Supported: docking + MD confirms binding; seizure models + flumazenil antagonism confirms mechanism

title: Mechanistic role of gut microbiota metabolites in hypertension-insomnia comorbidity via integrated network pharmacology and molecular dynamics
task: Elucidate gut microbiota metabolite role in hypertension-insomnia comorbidity via network pharmacology
key_results: 278 microbial metabolites mapped; 408 common targets identified; IL6 and PPARG as key hubs; IL-17 pathway central; 3-indolepropionic acid strongest IL6 binder (-5.472 kcal/mol)
comments: Integrative pipeline is comprehensive; however, mechanistic validation limited to in silico docking and drug-likeness prediction; in vivo or clinical validation absent.

model_io.model: Network pharmacology + molecular dynamics + MMTS analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gut metabolite dysregulation disrupts IL-17/lipid metabolism/neuroinflammation axis, linking hypertension-insomnia comorbidity; IL-17 inhibition and metabolite supplementation are therapeutic
hypotheses.result: Supported by network, pathway, and docking analyses; clinical translation remains unvalidated

title: Variance-calibrated cross-individual bootstrapping for small-sample neuroscience
task: Confidence interval estimation for small-sample neuroscience data
key_results: CIB-VC achieves ~95% coverage across skewed distributions; 23% narrower intervals than hierarchical bootstrapping on n=5 fish behavioral data
comments: Addresses a genuine gap in small-sample inference where conventional methods fail, though validation is limited to one biological dataset.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CIB-VC maintains nominal coverage under distributional skew better than stratified/hierarchical bootstrapping
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: CIB-VC ~95% coverage vs. stratified <1% under skew

title: Single cell profiling of ER stress in coronary artery disease and therapeutic mechanisms of Ginkgo biloba extract
task: Identify ER stress-related genes in coronary artery disease and validate therapeutic targets
key_results: 833 DEGs, 10 ERSRGs identified; Ginkgo biloba extract (GBE) validated as top therapeutic candidate; scRNA-seq confirms cell-type specificity of ERSRGs
comments: Comprehensive multi-omics integration (bulk RNA, scRNA, cell validation, qPCR) but lacks mechanistic depth on why GBE specifically regulates ER stress.

inputs: GEO gene expression data (CAD vs. healthy)

hypotheses.hypothesis: ER stress mediates immune dysregulation and angiogenesis in CAD pathogenesis
hypotheses.result: Supported by GSEA, GSVA, and immune deconvolution; GBE validated in vitro

title: Supervised machine learning intrusion detection review and multi-criteria evaluation
task: Review and rank supervised ML intrusion detection algorithms via multi-criteria decision-making
key_results: Tree-based methods (Random Forest, C4.5, Random Tree) rank highest; Naïve Bayes consistently poorest across KDD, NSL-KDD, CICIDS2017 datasets
comments: Standard benchmark study with transparent TOPSIS ranking; findings align with known tree-algorithm advantages in high-dimensional, feature-dependent data.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tree-based classifiers outperform linear/probabilistic methods on intrusion detection benchmarks under multiple evaluator preferences
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Random Forest, C4.5 top-ranked; Naïve Bayes consistently poor

title: Persistent cycles and network resilience: a hypernetwork-based framework for temporal graph analysis
task: Assess temporal network resilience via persistent cycle detection in hypernetworks
key_results: Temporal Cycle Number (TCN) and Temporal Cycle Ratio (TCR) metrics show stronger rank-association with disruption impact than static/temporal baselines across 6 domains
comments: Novel framework elegantly captures higher-order temporal redundancy; empirical validation is comprehensive but effect sizes and statistical significance testing not detailed.

model_io.model: Persistence-aware temporal hypernetwork construction and resilience evaluation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Persistent cycle closure (not motif persistence alone) predicts temporal network resilience better than degree-based metrics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: TCN/TCR outperform baselines; targeted removal of high-TCN nodes causes greater efficiency loss than degree-based attacks

title: Validation of conformal prediction in cervical atypia classification
task: Validate conformal prediction uncertainty quantification for cervical cancer classification
key_results: Expert-annotated validation shows conventional coverage metrics overestimate performance; conformal sets often misaligned with human labels; effective at OOD detection
comments: Critical methodological validation: conformal prediction's appeal to coverage obscures failure to exclude false-positive classes; human-alignment assessment is often overlooked.

model_io.model: Conformal prediction wrappers around deep learning classifiers
inputs: expert consensus labels from multiple annotators

hypotheses.hypothesis: Conformal prediction sets should align with human expert expectations, not just achieve statistical coverage
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: coverage validation insufficient; many sets include extraneous/unlikely classes

title: A contrastive learning framework with adaptive feature fusion for brain tumor classification
task: Classify brain tumors from MRI via contrastive learning with adaptive feature fusion
key_results: Achieves state-of-the-art performance on figshare dataset; dynamic label queue with multiple positive pairs reduces intra-class distance; adaptive feature fusion integrates multi-scale features
comments: Architecture is well-motivated; superior performance reported but limited to single dataset; cross-dataset generalization and clinical utility remain unaddressed.

model_io.model: Contrastive learning framework with adaptive feature fusion (AFF-CL)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multiple positive pairs from same-class images + adaptive feature fusion improve intra-class compactness and inter-class separation vs. standard contrastive learning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed on figshare dataset; outperforms existing methods; generalization to other datasets not tested

title: Characterization of T-cell receptor loci and expressed repertoire reveals a capacity for robust T-cell response in Atlantic cod (Gadus morhua)
task: Characterize T-cell receptor repertoire diversity in Atlantic cod despite MHCII/CD4 loss
key_results: Four TCR chains mapped; modest germline diversity with recent TRB expansion; surprisingly high expressed repertoire diversity (mostly private); untapped diversity via differential gene usage
comments: Well-executed genomic characterization; provides baseline for comparative immunology but mechanistic explanation for immune robustness despite gene loss remains unclear.

inputs: expressed TCR sequences (TRA, TRB, TRD, TRG chains)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Atlantic cod maintains immune response competency despite MHCII/CD4 loss via diverse TCR repertoire
hypotheses.result: Supported: high expressed repertoire diversity documented; mechanism remains unresolved

title: Screenathon 2.0: human–AI collaborative screening applied to patient-generated health data
task: Systematic review screening via human-AI collaborative classification
key_results: Screened 5,842 papers in 2 days across 27 institutions; identified 487 relevant records; post-processing critical for data quality
comments: Demonstrates practical crowdsourced AI collaboration but highlights that coverage alone is insufficient; false positives in prediction sets remain problematic.

model_io.model: Crowd-based ML screening with topic reallocation and noisy-label filtering

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI-assisted systematic reviewing increases trust and perceived usability among domain experts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by survey; exploratory analysis shows positive evaluations post-event

title: Real-time decentralized model predictive control for cooperative multi-robot object transport: experimental validation
task: Decentralized model predictive control for cooperative multi-robot object transport
key_results: Real-time deployment on two differential-drive robots with vision-based pose estimation; accurate trajectory tracking, strict constraint satisfaction across point-to-point, curvilinear, and obstacle-rich scenarios
comments: Experimental validation is thorough and real-world testbed integration is commendable; scalability beyond two robots and computational overhead scaling not explicitly addressed.

outputs: Local optimal control inputs for each robot

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decentralized MPC with adaptive weights achieves real-time cooperative transport with constraint satisfaction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed across experimental scenarios

title: Design of an in-pipe inspection robotic system (IPIRS) with YOLOv8–LSTM integration for real-time in-pipe navigation
task: Real-time in-pipe inspection robotic system with YOLOv8-LSTM for navigation and defect detection
key_results: YOLOv8 achieves mAP(0.5)=97.9% and F1=0.95 for object detection; LSTM achieves MSE=0.00037, MAE=0.00581 for motor voltage prediction; system autonomously navigates pipes 100–150 mm diameter using ROS, Gazebo, Rviz
comments: Strong detection and prediction accuracy with practical edge-deployment via ROS, but generalization to diverse pipe materials, occlusions, and real-world wear is not addressed.

outputs: Defect class and bounding box detections

inputs: IMU data (spatial scanning and group-action sequences)

hypotheses.hypothesis: YOLOv8-LSTM integration enables accurate real-time detection and navigation in curved pipes

title: A low-latency deep learning framework for volcanic ash cloud nowcasting using geostationary satellite imagery
task: Near-real-time deep learning nowcasting of volcanic ash dispersion from satellite imagery
key_results: Structural similarity index of 0.88 for 15-min forecasts; end-to-end edge workflow (download+inference) runs <5 seconds on NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin; scenario injection framework demonstrated at multiple scales (10 kt to 100 Mt)
comments: Strong operational performance on edge hardware, but scenario outputs are acknowledged as illustrative rather than validated predictions of actual nuclear plume morphology, limiting extrapolation.

inputs: SEVIRI Ash RGB composite satellite imagery

outputs: Augmented satellite imagery with synthetic plumes

hypotheses.hypothesis: Deep learning can achieve sub-5-second latency nowcasting on edge devices while maintaining predictive accuracy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SSIM=0.88 with <5s end-to-end latency

title: An advanced fermatean fuzzy DoC MCDM architecture for comprehensive quantitative assessment of physical fitness competency across academic institutions
task: Fermatean Fuzzy Deck-of-Cards MCDM for quantitative physical fitness assessment in academic institutions
key_results: Fermatean Fuzzy sets enable high-expressiveness representation of expert hesitation and dual uncertainty; Deck-of-Cards weighting produces rational, flexible prioritization of criteria (endurance, strength, flexibility, body composition, mental well-being); framework produces consistent, explainable rankings from incomplete information
comments: Novel integration of Fermatean Fuzzy sets with DoC weighting is theoretically motivated, but application is purely illustrative on a hypothetical case study—real institutional data validation is lacking.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fermatean Fuzzy sets with DoC weighting provide more expressive uncertainty modeling than classical fuzzy/intuitionistic fuzzy/Pythagorean fuzzy sets
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; higher expressiveness for expert hesitation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Framework generates consistent and explainable fitness competency rankings from uncertain expert opinions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed on hypothetical case study

title: Lightweight FMCW radar framework for human activity recognition under limited data conditions
task: Lightweight FMCW radar-based human activity recognition under limited data conditions
key_results: Modified ResNet-18 with depthwise separable convolutions and bi-LSTM achieves 91.98% accuracy and 89.82% F1-score on 7 daily activities; framework processes Range-Doppler, Range-Azimuth, Range-Elevation as structured vectors; data augmentation (spatial shifting, intensity scaling, Doppler flipping, noise) improves generalization
comments: Practical lightweight architecture with strong cross-scene and leave-one-person-out results; however, limited to 60 GHz radar and 7 activities—transferability to other frequency bands and broader activity sets unclear.

inputs: Range-Doppler, Range-Azimuth, Range-Elevation feature maps from 60 GHz FMCW radar

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lightweight deep learning with spatial-temporal feature extraction enables accurate HAR under limited data with edge-device efficiency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 91.98% accuracy, 89.82% F1; superior cross-scene and leave-one-person-out performance

title: General two-level framework for demand-responsive transport optimization
task: Demand-responsive transport optimization with electric vehicles and special requests
key_results: Three model variants (General, Sections, Routes) tested on Rzeszow public transport system dataset (64 bus stops, 200 passengers); framework confirmed useful with and without extensions (EV charging, dedicated spaces, Wi-Fi availability)
comments: Practical relevance demonstrated but performance metrics (optimality gap, computational time, cost savings) are not quantitatively reported, limiting assessment of effectiveness.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Generalized DRT framework with extensions (EV charging, dedicated spaces, Wi-Fi) is effective on realistic public transport data
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; model tested and confirmed useful

title: Deep maximum margin matrix factorization
task: Deep maximum margin matrix factorization for collaborative filtering with ordinal feedback
key_results: Deep MMMF variant outperforms state-of-the-art CF methods on three benchmark datasets; improvement measured by Normalized Mean Absolute Error (NMAE); non-linear user-feature modeling supersedes linear hyperplane restriction
comments: Conceptually sound extension of MMMF to deep architectures, but specific NMAE improvements and benchmark datasets are not quantitatively stated in abstract.

inputs: Partially observed user-item interaction matrix (ordinal ratings)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Non-linear user feature representations in deep MMMF outperform linear hyperplane-based MMMF
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; superior performance on benchmark datasets vs. state-of-the-art

title: A misclassification-aware explainable hybrid CNN-vision transformer framework for radiographic weld inspection
task: Hybrid CNN-Vision Transformer for radiographic weld defect classification with explainability
key_results: Hybrid CNN-ViT achieves 98.56% accuracy (vs. 97.90% CNN baseline), ~31% relative reduction in misclassification rate on RIAWELC dataset; strong generalization on external GDXray dataset; Grad-CAM and transformer self-attention maps show global contextual modeling enhances discrimination of visually similar defects
comments: Strong accuracy and interpretability via explainability methods; the 31% misclassification reduction is impressive, though absolute gap (0.66%) is modest, warranting validation on larger and more diverse datasets.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hybrid CNN-ViT with global context modeling discriminates visually similar weld defects better than pure CNN
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 98.56% vs. 97.90%, ~31% misclassification reduction

hypotheses.hypothesis: Model generalizes to out-of-distribution external data (GDXray)
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; good performance on GDXray dataset

title: Human-centric participation paradigm: exploring the enjoyment experience of cultural heritage virtual museum based on system usability and VR technology
task: Explaining user enjoyment in virtual cultural heritage museums through psychological constructs
key_results: Immersion has strongest effect on enjoyment; basic psychological needs moderate perceived usefulness-enjoyment link; PLS-SEM analysis of 630 Virtual Palace Museum users
comments: Well-grounded in established theories (HMSAM, SDT) but the moderate effect sizes and non-significance of control suggest the model may underspecify other contributing factors.

hypotheses.hypothesis: System usability enhances psychological responses (perceived usefulness, curiosity, joy, control, immersion)
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; significant direct effects observed

hypotheses.hypothesis: Immersion is strongest predictor of enjoyment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed

hypotheses.hypothesis: Control significantly impacts enjoyment
hypotheses.result: Rejected; no significant effect

title: A quantile-based composite ionospheric disturbance estimator for RTK positioning reliability
task: Ionospheric disturbance estimation for RTK positioning reliability using GNSS observables
key_results: Quantile-based composite disturbance estimator integrates rolling-median ROTI and VTEC gradient information; validated on dense GNSS network (Latvia); method is GNSS-independent, requires only ionospheric observables, and provides physically interpretable risk scores
comments: Methodologically sound and reproducible, but validation limited to mid-latitude region (Latvia); extension to sparse networks and other ionospheric regimes acknowledged as requiring further investigation.

inputs: GNSS-derived Vertical TEC (VTEC) field and gradients

hypotheses.hypothesis: Composite estimator combining temporal and spatial ionospheric disturbance measures outperforms single-parameter indicators
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; captures patterns not resolved by global ionospheric models

title: Experimental and Monte Carlo simulation study on photons shielding properties of ZrO2-reinforced polyester composites utilizing GEANT4 and MCNP codes
task: Gamma-ray shielding performance of polyester/ZrO2 composites
key_results: Good agreement between Monte Carlo simulations (GEANT4, MCNPX) and experimental data at 662 keV; lead equivalent thickness increases monotonically with ZrO2 filler content
comments: Multi-code validation strengthens confidence, but evaluation limited to single isotope source; broader energy coverage would better establish applicability range.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Increasing ZrO2 filler content improves gamma-ray shielding performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; systematic improvement in LAC, MAC, HVL, and lead equivalent thickness with ZrO2 loading

title: Decoupling in a joint communication and sensing system with metasurface
task: Self-interference decoupling in joint communication and sensing systems via metasurface
key_results: 2×2 patch antenna array with modified split-ring resonator metasurface at 9–10 GHz; hardware–algorithm co-optimization combining metasurface suppression and null-space projection beamforming
comments: Proof-of-concept demonstrates promise but scalability to larger MIMO arrays and broader frequency ranges not addressed.

inputs: transmitted waveforms from TX antenna array
received signals at RX antenna array

hypotheses.hypothesis: Modified split-ring resonator metasurface reduces coupling between TX and RX arrays in compact MIMO
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through fabrication and measurement at 9–10 GHz
